Saturday, Jul 4 · 11:00 AM to 2:00 PM GMT+1

Cafe One by Sterling

Commercial Ave · Lagos, LA

Lagos dbt Meetup - July 2026 (in person)

Hosted by David A.

Lagos dbt Meetup
4.6
54 reviews
Details

We’re excited to host our July IN-PERSON Lagos dbt Meetup in 2026, organized by the Young Data Professionals Community! We look forward to learning, socializing, and seeing both new and familiar faces.

Our venue has limited capacity, so please only RSVP if you intend to attend. If you need to cancel at the last minute, kindly update your RSVP status on Meetup to “Not Going.”

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

We welcome data analysts, analytics engineers, data scientists, data engineers, data architects, and more.
🇳🇬 For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-nigeria channel in dbt Slack. Join the dbt Community on Slack using the signup instructions here: https://slack.getdbt.com/

🏠Venue: Cafe One by Sterling Commercial Avenue, Yaba, Lagos.

✨Agenda✨
* Welcome and Introductions
* Talk 1 - "Beyond the Prompt: Powering Multi-Agent RAG Systems with the dbt Semantic Layer" by Israel Odeajo, AI Engineer
* Talk 2 - "From Data Graveyard to Operational Backbone: Why the Modern Data Stack Needs Contracts, Not Just Pipelines" by Oluwasijibomi Ogunniran, Lead Data Engineer
* Break Networking and refreshments
* Talk 3 - "Metric by Integrity by Default" by Adedamola Onabanjo, Data Professional

Do you have questions for the session? Please use this link

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

----------------------------------
dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 30,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Read more

events.page_title: Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs), Thu, Nov 21, 2024, 8:00 PM | Meetup
events.raw_text:
Skip to content
Homepage

Thu, Nov 21, 2024 · 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM CET

Online event

This event has passed

Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Lagos dbt Meetup
4.6
54 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Speaker: Alex Welch, Head of Data @dbt Labs
Topic: BI Tools & Semantic Layer
When: June 27 @ 4pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Whether you’re looking at flight data, sales figures, consumer transactions, or biometric stats — access to the correct sources + a well-trained user + BI tools + dbt = data that works!!
This month we have Alex Welch, Head of Data at dbt Labs, to answer questions and discuss more about BI tools, analytics trends & the Semantic Layer!

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-merge channel in Slack to participate live!

events.raw_text:
Skip to content

Sat, Mar 2, 2024 · 11:00 AM to 2:00 PM GMT+1

Cafe One by Sterling

Commercial Ave · Lagos, LA

Lagos dbt Meetup #5 (in-person)

Hosted by FABIYI O. and dbt L.

dbt L. is a Super Organizer

Lagos dbt Meetup
4.6
54 reviews
Details

We're excited to host our first IN-PERSON Lagos dbt meetup in 2024, organized by Young Data Professionals Community. Looking forward to learning, socializing, and seeing new and familiar faces.

Our venue has capacity limits, so please only RSVP if you intend to come. If you need to cancel last minute or change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going."

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, expect big-picture conversations related to the data stacks, data operations, data teams, modeling, testing, dashboards, etc. If you work with data, this group is for you! We welcome data analysts, scientists, engineers, architects, and more!!

🇳🇬For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-nigeria channel in dbt Slack. Join the dbt Community on the Slack platform. Signup instructions are here: https://slack.getdbt.com/

🏠Venue: Cafe One by Sterling, Commercial Ave, Sabo yaba, Lagos 101245, Lagos.

✨Agenda✨

* Welcome and Introductions

* Talk 1 - Unlocking the Power of Incremental Models in dbt Cloud: A Cost-Effective Strategy by Adedamola Onabanjo, Business Intelligence Manager at Kuda.

* Talk 2 - Practical Q & A on ETL Best Practices by Ahmed Oladapo, Senior Data Engineer at Pangaea.

* Break & Networking

* Talk 3 - Unlocking New Possibilities: Integrating DBT with Microsoft Fabric by Emmanuel Ikpesu, Data Scientist at AB InBev

* Networking and refreshments

Presentation #1: Unlocking the Power of Incremental Models in dbt Cloud: A Cost-Effective Strategy
The session will provide insights into the challenges faced before embracing incremental models, the meticulous research strategies applied in choosing the right fix based on data types, business and technical stakeholder needs, and data synchronization frequency. Additionally, I'll share the configuration strategy implemented, lessons learned along the journey, and our current status with incremental models.

Presentation #2: Practical Q & A on ETL Best Practices
During this session, Ahmed will answer questions about ETL best practices and how to deal with common ETL challenges.

Presentation #3: Unlocking New Possibilities: Integrating dbt with Microsoft Fabric
Our objective is to empower data professionals with the knowledge and skills to leverage the strengths of both dbt and Microsoft Fabric. By the end of this session, participants will be well-equipped to enhance their data workflows, combining the advanced data transformation capabilities of dbt with the comprehensive data solution offered by Microsoft Fabric.

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/

🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-nigeria channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------

dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Read more

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, expect big-picture conversations related to the data stacks, data operations, data teams, modeling, testing, dashboards, etc. If you work with data, this group is for you! We welcome data analysts, scientists, engineers, architects, and more!!

🇳🇬For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-nigeria channel in dbt Slack. Join the dbt Community on the Slack platform. Signup instructions are here: https://slack.getdbt.com/

🏠Venue: (DSN) Data Scientists Network /Data Science Nigeria -AI HUB 2

33 Queen St · Lagos, LA

✨Agenda✨

* Welcome and Introductions

* Talk 1 - Data architecture - Maurice Singa, Business System Associate at Sun King

* Talk 2 - Unlocking Insights: Analytical Databases vs. Transactional Databases - A Deep Dive with DuckDB - Ogunseye Oluwajuwon Micheal, Data Analyst at Obran Cooperative

* Break & Networking

* Panel Session - "Is everyone's data (infrastructure) messy & how does dbt help?"

* Networking and refreshments

Presentation #1: Data architecture by Maurice Singa, Business System Associate at Sun King
In this talk, I will discuss the design principles of systems to support the evolving data needs of an enterprise.

Presentation #2: Unlocking Insights: Analytical Databases vs. Transactional Databases - A Deep Dive with DuckDB by Ogunseye Oluwajuwon Micheal, Data Analyst at Obran Cooperative
In this engaging presentation, I'll embark on a journey to help you understand why data analysts need to expand their horizons and advocate for the adoption of analytical databases in their workplaces. By exploring the benefits of analytical databases through the lens of DuckDB, I'll showcase how these databases are designed to deliver lightning-fast performance, enabling data teams to glean insights from vast amounts of data with ease.

Panel Session: "Is everyone's data (infrastructure) messy & how does dbt help?"
In this engaging panel session, the panelists will be discussing the state of data and data infrastructure in organizations and how this can be improved especially using dbt.

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

Coalesce: The Analytics Engineering Conference
Network event

183 attendees from 16 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Lagos dbt Meetup
4.6
54 reviews
Details

In case you missed it, dbt Labs is hosting Coalesce 2022 from October 17 - 21. It is the annual conference dedicated to advancing the practice of analytics engineering. Coalesce offers data practitioners an opportunity to deepen their knowledge and network. You can choose your own adventure including in-person, online, and hybrid experiences.

Coalesce Online (FREE) | October 17-21, 2022. The online experience that you know and love. Connect with data practitioners around the globe in sessions designed for North America, APAC, EMEA and LATAM regions. Catch live stream sessions from Coalesce New Orleans, interact with the community and speakers via Slack, and attend exclusively virtual sessions.
Coalesce New Orleans | October 17-21, 2022 | Located at the Hyatt Regency, New Orleans. Our first time together, in person! Attend a presentation, workshop, or panel discussion. Participate in activities, a hackathon, intimate discussions, and social activities. Meet community members and partners while exploring the history and culture of New Orleans.
Coalesce Online + | London, 20 October 2022 | Sydney, 21 October 2022. A one-day event taking place in London and Sydney, complementing Coalesce Online. This will include tech talks, hosted discussions, an Executive keynote and a post-event networking session.

⚠️Please note that Coalesce 2022 won't be hosted on Meetup.com.
✅REGISTER + SIGN UP for sessions here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com
📅Review the schedule here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com/schedule
⭐As a member of the dbt Meetup Network, please use your discount code MEETUP15 for Premium and GA tickets.

We're very excited to be hosting our first IN-PERSON Lagos dbt meetup ever. Looking forward to socializing and seeing new and familiar faces.

Our venue has capacity limits, so please only RSVP if you intend to com. If you need to cancel last minute or change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going."

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🇳🇬For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-nigeria channel in dbt Slack. Join the dbt Community on the Slack platform. Signup instructions are here: https://slack.getdbt.com/

🏠Venue: DSN AI Hub 2 - 33 Queen St, Alagomeji Yaba.

✨Agenda✨
9:30am - 10:00am (SWAGs) (Registration / welcoming guests)
10:00am - 10:10am | Welcome Speech (Opeyemi Fabiyi)
10:10 -10:15 Intros and words from dbt Labs team (Amada Echeverria)
10:15am - 10:25am | Meet someone new (Moyinoluwa Anoma)
10:25- 10:45 | Talk #1 - 🗣️ David Effiong, Analytics Engineer @ Mecho Autotech (Talk: 15mins + Q&A: 5min)
10:45am - 11:00am | Ice break/ Game (Moyinoluwa Anoma)
11:00am - 11:20am | Talk #2 - 🗣️ Favour Oyelami, Senior Data Scientists @ Bluechip Technologies Limited (Talk: 15mins + Q&A: 5min)
11:20am - 11:50am | Game /Food (Moyinoluwa Anoma)
11:50am - 12:20pm | Panel session (moderated by Opeyemi Fabiyi) with the panelists below. 🗣️

Abisola Oni - Data Strategist @ Paystack
Paschal Chukwuemeka - Head of Data @ Kobo360
Oliseamaka Chiedu - Deputy Lead, Data Analytics @ Flutterwave

12:20pm - 12:30pm | Closing (Opeyemi Fabiyi)

dbt Labs Meetup Policy
Any in-person gathering or meeting holds an inherent risks, including exposure to COVID-19 and other serious, easily-transmittable viruses or diseases (an “Illness”). By attending this event, I agree to voluntarily assume all risk related to attending the event, including but not limited to exposure to Illness and agree to not hold dbt Labs or any of their affiliates including partners and sponsors, directors, officers, employees, agents, contractors, volunteers (“Representatives”) or venues liable for Illness or other harms other than those resulting from dbt Lab’s gross negligence or willful misconduct. I understand and agree that dbt Labs has an interest and obligation in protecting the health and well-being of dbt Labs. Representatives, venue staff and other attendees; therefore, I agree that, if I’ve chosen not to be fully vaccinated as determined by the CDC or by applicable local public health guidelines for personal, religious, medical or other reasons, I will only participate in a dbt Labs meetup remotely by watching live or via a recording, and I will not attend in-person. When in attendance in person, I will take necessary precautions while at the event in accordance with local and national health guidelines and regulations, which may include, but not limited to, engaging in appropriate social distancing, wearing a mask in public areas when not consuming food or beverages, minimizing face touching, frequently washing hands and avoiding risky environments immediately preceding the meeting, such as overcrowded bars or restaurants. I agree to not attend any dbt Labs event if I feel ill or have had recent exposure to an individual who may be (or is) infected with an Illness. The building or venue may have other covid- or public health-related requirements in place, and I agree to abide by those requirements when disclosed by venue. I agree that dbt Labs, by hosting an event, has not undertaken any obligation to provide an environment free of any Illness, cannot reasonably be expected to enforce covid- or public health-related requirements, must rely on the compliance of attendees, and cannot police the behavior of attendees, venue staff, or third parties. I agree that dbt Labs hosting an event does not give rise to dbt Labs being liable for any damages whatsoever in any form, including but not limited to, consequential, indirect, or special damages. By attending, you consent to dbt Labs’ use and sharing of your reply and/or registrant information for purposes of planning for, hosting, and administering meetups. If I have any privacy-related questions or want to express concerns, request deletion, or exercise any other privacy rights, I understand I may contact dbt Labs at privacy@dbtlabs.com.

✨We're excited to host our second virtual dbt Meetup in Nigeria!✨
➡️ Join our the dbt Slack community:
https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝🏽 Be part of the conversation in the #local-nigeria channel in dbt Slack! This is where the chat will also take place during meetups.

✨ Speakers✨
🎤Ayodele Babatunde is a Data Engineer at Critical Techworks. LinkedIn : https://www.linkedin.com/in/bammy2050/

🎤Oluwabukunmi Oladayo Ige is an Incoming Data Engineer at Paystack. LinkedIn : https://www.linkedin.com/in/oluwabukunmi-ige-98957a105/
-----------------------------------
✨ What to expect at this meetup ✨
✅Additional details to follow - stay tuned!
5:00 - Welcome Remarks
5:05 - Implementing CI/CD best practices on dbt + Q&A with Ayodele Babatunde
5:35 - Data Modeling and its role in your Data Stack + Q&A with Oluwabukunmi Ige
6:05 - Closing Remarks by Opeyemi Fabiyi
6:10 - Feedback session & Optional chat

-----------------------------------
dbt Meetups are gatherings dedicated to helping you own your analytics engineering workflow. Events will focus on the impact of dbt (data build tool) in the data community and professional development tools for analytics engineers.
Expect big-picture conversations related to the data stacks, data operations, data teams, modeling, testing, dashboards, etc.
If you work with data, this group is for you! We welcome data analysts, scientists, engineers, architects, and more!!

Dear dbt Community,

In case you missed it, we are hosting Coalesce 2021 from December 6 - 10. This is a FREE online Analytics Engineering Conference where you can connect with over 75 data leaders presenting panels, workshops, activations—and more—exploring today’s analytics engineering ecosystem.

⚠️Please note that 2021 Coalesce won't be hosted on Meetup.com. ✅REGISTER + SIGN UP for sessions here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com
📅Subscribe to Coalesce Calendar to automatically add sessions to your personal calendar: https://www.addevent.com/calendar/gL298333

We look forward to connecting with you at Coalesce! ✨
